back_translation: When Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had captured Ai and devoted it to destruction — that as he had done to Jericho and its king, so he had done to Ai and its king — and that the inhabitants of Gibeon had made peace with Israel and were living among them,
they feared greatly, because Gibeon was a great city, like one of the royal cities; and because it was larger than Ai, and all its men were warriors.
So Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent word to Hoham king of Hebron, to Piram king of Jarmuth, to Japhia king of Lachish, and to Debir king of Eglon, saying,
'Come up to me and help me, and we will strike Gibeon, because it has made peace with Joshua and with the sons of Israel.'

So the five kings of the Amorites — the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon — gathered and went up, they and all their armies; they camped against Gibeon and made war against it.

The men of Gibeon sent to Joshua at the camp at Gilgal, saying, 'Do not loosen your hands from your servants. Come up to us quickly and save us and help us, for all the kings of the Amorites who live in the hill country have gathered against us.'
So Joshua went up from Gilgal — he and all the troops of war with him, and all the valiant warriors.
The LORD said to Joshua, 'Do not fear them, for into your hand I have given them. Not one of them shall stand before you.'
Joshua came upon them suddenly — all night he had gone up from Gilgal.

And the LORD threw them into confusion before Israel, and He struck them with a great blow at Gibeon, and pursued them along the ascent of Beth-horon, and struck them as far as Azekah and as far as Makkedah.

And as they fled before Israel, on the descent of Beth-horon, the LORD cast down on them large stones from heaven as far as Azekah, and they died. Many died from the hailstones, more than the sons of Israel killed with the sword.

Then Joshua spoke to the LORD on the day that the LORD gave the Amorites before the sons of Israel, and he said before the eyes of Israel, 'Sun, stand still over Gibeon, and moon over the Valley of Aijalon.'

So the sun stood still and the moon stopped until the nation took vengeance on its enemies. Is this not written in the Book of Jashar? The sun stopped in the middle of the sky and did not hasten to set for about a full day.
There was no day like that one — before it or after it — when the LORD listened to the voice of a man, because the LORD fought for Israel.
Then Joshua and all Israel with him returned to the camp at Gilgal.
Meanwhile, these five kings had fled and hidden themselves in the cave at Makkedah.
And it was reported to Joshua: 'The five kings have been found, hiding in the cave at Makkedah.'
Joshua said, 'Roll large stones against the mouth of the cave, and post men over it to guard them.
But you, do not stop. Pursue after your enemies and attack their rear. Do not let them reach their cities, for the LORD your God has given them into your hand.'
When Joshua and the sons of Israel had finished striking them with a very great blow until they were finished, and the survivors who remained from them came to the fortified cities,
all the people returned to the camp to Joshua at Makkedah in peace. No one sharpened his tongue against any of the sons of Israel.
Then Joshua said, 'Open the mouth of the cave and bring those five kings out from the cave to me.'
So they did, and brought out those five kings from the cave to him — the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon.

When they had brought those kings out to Joshua, Joshua called to all the men of Israel and said to the commanders of the men of war who had gone with him, 'Come near, put your feet on the necks of these kings.' So they came near and put their feet on their necks.
Joshua said to them, 'Do not fear and do not be dismayed. Be strong and courageous, for thus will the LORD do to all your enemies whom you fight.'
After this Joshua struck them and killed them, and hung them on five trees, and they were hanging on the trees until evening.

At the time of sunset Joshua commanded, and they took them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had hidden, and they placed large stones over the mouth of the cave — to this very day.

Joshua captured Makkedah on that day and struck it with the edge of the sword, along with its king. He devoted them to destruction — and all the souls who were in it; he did not leave a survivor. He did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Makkedah to Libnah and fought against Libnah.

And the LORD also gave it into the hand of Israel, with its king. He struck it with the edge of the sword, and all the souls who were in it; he did not leave in it a survivor. He did to its king as he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Libnah to Lachish. He camped against it and fought against it.

And the LORD gave Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he captured it on the second day. He struck it with the edge of the sword, and all the souls who were in it, according to all that he had done to Libnah.
Then Horam king of Gezer went up to help Lachish, but Joshua struck him and his people until no survivor was left to him.
Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Lachish to Eglon. They camped against it and fought against it.
They captured it on that day and struck it with the edge of the sword. All the souls in it on that day he devoted to destruction, according to all that he had done to Lachish.
Joshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron and fought against it.

They captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword — its king, all its cities, and all the souls who were in it; he did not leave a survivor, according to all that he had done to Eglon. He devoted it to destruction along with all the souls who were in it.
Joshua and all Israel with him turned to Debir and fought against it.

He captured it, its king, and all its cities. They struck them with the edge of the sword and devoted to destruction all the souls who were in it; he did not leave a survivor. As he had done to Hebron, so he did to Debir and its king, and as he had done to Libnah and its king.

So Joshua struck all the land — the hill country, the Negev, the lowland, and the slopes — and all their kings. He did not leave a survivor, but every breath he devoted to destruction, as the LORD, the God of Israel, had commanded.
Joshua struck them from Kadesh-barnea as far as Gaza, and all the land of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
All these kings and their land Joshua captured in one time (campaign), because the LORD, the God of Israel, fought for Israel.
Then Joshua and all Israel with him returned to the camp at Gilgal.
